input:
May sends no warning. The dogs of hesychasm breeze through the hall past cameras and lasers, tearing the music from the stand. Each breath swells and tips the pier, every heartbeat bears a plank above the churn. Feelers fold up in Casimir spaces beneath the cannonade of her seashell eyes, a gull rests its eyes in the morning's pastel mercy. Have we passed Utnapishtim's station? Where was salvation? Lord Jesus Christ, have mercy on me, a sinner.

Time heals all wounds, exposed, pussing, doused in alcohol, hazy faces hovering, putting protective instincts and hidden aggressions to bed in the forbidden desert, hoping it was all benign.
